'''Dean Gaskell''' (born 12 April 1983) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Gaskell was born in England, and he is of Irish descent. He currently resides in [[Wigan]]

==Career==
Gaskell played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in [[RFL Championship|National League One]]. He also played for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]].

Dean Gaskell's position of choice was on the {{rlp|wg}}, but he could also operate as a {{rlp|fb}}.

Gaskell had previously played for the [[Leigh Centurions]], and had [[Super League]] experience with the [[Warrington Wolves]].

Dean Gaskell was successful with [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], in 2007 scoring 10 tries in 14 games.<ref name="Player Stats">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|title=Player Stats|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-20|accessdate=2007-07-20|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070621200401/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/playerstats.php|archivedate=2007-06-21|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Gaskell is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international, however he missed the winter programme and the games against [[Russia national rugby league team|Russia]] and [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]] through injury.<ref name="Dean Gaskell">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=11|title=Dean Gaskell|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-20|accessdate=2007-07-20|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201412/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=11|archivedate=2007-09-27|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Ireland training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02|archive-date=14 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200414090730/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref>

After Rugby League, he had a short but successful stint in [[ Mixed Martial Arts| MMA ]], with a record of 5 wins and 1 loss.
